lede: A look at April 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including The Rizzler and Samuel C. Morrison Jr., and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2020 Nova Scotia attacks
events.desc: A killing spree in Nova Scotia, Canada, leaves 22 people and the perpetrator dead, making it the deadliest rampage in the country's history.

events.title: 2005 conclave
events.desc: Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger is elected to the papacy and becomes Pope Benedict XVI.

events.title: Air Philippines Flight 541
events.desc: Air Philippines Flight 541 crashes in Samal, Davao del Norte, killing all 131 people on board.

events.title: Oklahoma City bombing
events.desc: Oklahoma City bombing: The Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City, US, is bombed, killing 168 people including 19 children under the age of six.

events.title: Gun turret
events.desc: A gun turret explodes on the USS Iowa, killing 47 sailors.

events.title: Salyut 1
events.desc: Launch of Salyut 1, the first space station.

events.title: Grace Kelly
events.desc: Actress Grace Kelly marries Prince Rainier of Monaco.

events.title: Mae West
events.desc: Mae West is sentenced to ten days in jail for obscenity for her play Sex.

events.title: Kishinev pogrom
events.desc: The Kishinev pogrom in Kishinev (Bessarabia) begins, forcing tens of thousands of Jews to later seek refuge in Palestine and the Western world.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Baltimore riot of 1861: A pro-Secession mob in Baltimore attacks United States Army troops marching through the city.

births.name: Samuel C. Morrison Jr.
births.line: Jr., Liberian-American journalist, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Getúlio Vargas
births.line: Brazilian lawyer and politician, 14th President of Brazil (died 1954)

deaths.text: George Ariyoshi, American lawyer and politician, 3rd Governor of Hawaii
Martin Conway, 1st Baron Conway of Allington, English cartographer and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ælfheah of Canterbury (Anglican, Catholic, Orthodox)
Christian feast day: Olaus and Laurentius Petri (Lutheran)
